articles.title: JPMorgan Chase unveils $50 billion buyback, Goldman Sachs raises dividend after Fed stress test
articles.summary: The announcements followed the release of the Federal Reserve's annual stress test, which found that all 32 large banks weathered a hypothetical recession.

articles.title: NBA to begin naming winning bids for Europe teams in the coming months, deputy commissioner says
articles.summary: The NBA plans to begin naming winning bidders for 12 permanent European teams in the next 60 to 90 days, Deputy Commissioner Mark Tatum told CNBC.

articles.title: California’s counting on an IPO tax windfall. Several factors are complicating the equation
articles.summary: The IPOs of SpaceX, OpenAI and Anthropic could create a tax windfall for the state of California. But experts say the revenue impact may be blunted.

articles.title: Hollister partners with Target to tap into $89 billion back-to-college shopping market
articles.summary: Hollister's partnership with Target comes as both companies look to capitalize on the lucrative back-to-college shopping market.

articles.title: SNAP restrictions could change what shoppers buy — and food giants are watching
articles.summary: SNAP food restrictions are spreading to more states, pressuring major food and beverage as consumers shift spending away from soda, candy and processed foods.

articles.title: 'Reward for failure': Investor support for Target Chair Brian Cornell falls to lowest level ever
articles.summary: Target Chair Brian Cornell built the retailer into a $100 billion-plus giant, but recent struggles and underperformance have fueled investor calls for change.

articles.title: Lucid to lay off roughly 18% of U.S. workforce, COO Marc Winterhoff leaves
articles.summary: Lucid Group is cutting its U.S. workforce by 18% as part of a cost-savings plan. It also said COO Marc Winterhoff is departing effective immediately.

articles.title: UPS to invest $48 million in temperature-controlled facilities amid healthcare boom
articles.summary: UPS is announcing a new investment in healthcare logistics as demand for the temperature-controlled category booms, CNBC has learned exclusively.

articles.title: Fala upałów uderza w Polskę i Europę. Gospodarka też może ucierpieć
articles.summary: Polska w najbliższych dniach będzie zmagać się z falą upałów. To oznacza gigantyczne koszty dla gospodarki w całej Europie. Nic nie wskazuje jednak na to, by groziła nam powtórka z blackoutu w 2015 r. — System jest przygotowany — zapewnia Maciej Wapiński, rzecznik prasowy Polskich Sieci Elektroenergetycznych.
